				'name'                => sanitize_text_field( $this->name ),
				'thirdParty'          => (bool) $this->sharesData,
				//legacy, sharesData was first called third party.
				'sharesData'          => (bool) $this->sharesData,
				//fluid upgrade
				'secondParty'         => (bool) $this->secondParty,
				'sync'                => (bool) $this->sync,

				'slug'                => empty($this->slug) ? '' : sanitize_title( $this->slug ),

			)

extra.message: REST route registered without a permission_callback. This defaults to open access in older WordPress versions. Always define a permission_callback that verifies user capabilities.
Passing a variable directly to $wpdb query methods without prepare() may allow SQL injection. Use $wpdb->prepare() to safely parameterize queries.

unserialize() called without the allowed_classes option set to false (or a whitelist). Without this restriction, all classes available in the autoloader may be instantiated, enabling Object Injection attacks. Use unserialize($data, ['allowed_classes' => false]).
